original_prompt:
Hey there! I'm heading to Paris on Monday and I need your help finding a restaurant that's open on Mondays, serves Chinese food, and won't break the bank (under 34 euros per person).
Can you suggest a restaurant with the best rating and reviews.
Also, I'm looking for a car rental company with prices under 50 euros. Could you recommend a few options and let me know which one has the best rating?
Please share the names of the recommended restaurant and car rental company. Thanks a bunch!
assessment_reasoning: The user prompt is mostly clear: it specifies the city (Paris), day (Monday), cuisine (Chinese), a hard price ceiling for restaurants (< €34 per person), and a price ceiling for car rentals (< €50). It also requests the highest-rated restaurant (and reviews) matching those restaurant constraints and a few car-rental options with the best-rated one highlighted. Minor ambiguities remain: “best rating and reviews” doesn’t define how to combine rating vs. review count or sentiment; “prices under 50 euros” doesn’t explicitly state timeframe (per day, per rental) though per-day is typical; “a few options” doesn’t specify how many; tie-breaking rules (if multiple entities share the top rating) are not stated. Because these are small execution details but the core task and constraints are well defined, I rate it a 4 (Slightly Ambiguous).

variations.L1: I'm going to Paris and need help with food and transportation. Can you find a place to eat and a car rental for me? I want something inexpensive — just suggest a few options and tell me which ones seem best. Thanks!
variations.L2: I'm heading to Paris on Monday. I'm looking for a Chinese restaurant that's inexpensive (around or under €34 per person) and some car rental options that are under €50 per day. Can you recommend a few restaurants and car rental companies?
variations.L3: I'll be in Paris on Monday. Please find Chinese restaurants in Paris that are open on Mondays and cost less than €34 per person; pick the one with the highest rating (include any review summary if available). Also list several car rental companies in Paris with daily rates under €50, and indicate which of those has the best rating. Share the names of the recommended restaurant and the top-rated car rental.
variations.L4: Hey there! I'm heading to Paris on Monday and I need your help finding a restaurant that's open on Mondays, serves Chinese food, and won't break the bank (under 34 euros per person). Can you suggest a restaurant with the best rating and reviews? Also, I'm looking for a car rental company with prices under 50 euros. Could you recommend a few options and let me know which one has the best rating? Please share the names of the recommended restaurant and car rental company. Thanks a bunch!
variations.L5: I need a precise selection for a trip to Paris. Follow these steps and return the final names and ratings: 1) Call get_all_restaurants_in_city(city='Paris'). 2) From those results, use check_restaurant_opening_hours(restaurant_names=[...]) and keep only restaurants open on 'Monday'. 3) Use get_cuisine_type_for_restaurants(restaurant_names=[...]) and keep only cuisine_type == 'Chinese'. 4) Use get_price_for_restaurants(restaurant_names=[...]) and keep only price_per_person < 34. 5) Use get_rating_reviews_for_restaurants(restaurant_names=[...]) and select the restaurant(s) with the maximum rating (include rating and review summary). 6) For cars: call get_all_car_rental_companies_in_city(city='Paris'), then get_car_price_per_day(company_name=[...]) and keep companies with price_per_day < 50. 7) Call get_rating_reviews_for_car_rental(company_name=[...]) and select the company(ies) with the highest rating. Return a JSON object exactly like: { "restaurant": {"name": "...", "rating": ..., "review_summary": "..."}, "car_rental": {"name": "...", "rating": ..., "review_summary": "...", "other_recommendations": [{"name":"...","price":...}, ...] } }
